title: Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Abuse Resistance · Claim Audit: Privileged Operation
summary: Authenticated users can change identifiers, cross tenant boundaries, automate expensive actions, and exploit support or administrator workflows. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 privileged operation increment in deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.
Model automation, scraping, spam, enumeration, resource exhaustion, fraud, and moderator burden with proportional controls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:

# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.

release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]

transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Privileged Operation · Theory Privileged Operation
steps.instruction:
theory case — An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Privileged Operation contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Privileged Operation · Theory Privileged Operation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work. For Predict Privileged Operation · Theory Privileged Operation, keep Privileged Operation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The worked example variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work. A defensible solution applies Privileged Operation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite.
